label: Africa
description: The continent south of the Sahara plus the Nile valley — the birthplace of humanity and of the oldest continuous cultural traditions.

label: The Americas
description: North, Central, and South America — from the first Paleoindians through the Mesoamerican and Andean civilizations to the modern hemispheric powers.

label: East Asia
description: China, Korea, Japan, and mainland Southeast Asia — a cultural sphere anchored by the succession of Chinese dynasties.

label: South & Central Asia
description: The Indian subcontinent and the Eurasian steppe — home to the Mauryas, Guptas, Mughals, Timurids, and countless nomadic empires.

label: Middle East & North Africa
description: Mesopotamia, the Levant, Arabia, Persia, Anatolia, Egypt, and the Maghreb — the crossroads where writing, religion, and empire were repeatedly reinvented.

label: Europe
description: From Iberia to the Urals — Greco-Roman antiquity, Christendom, the Renaissance, industrial revolution, and the modern European project.

label: Oceania
description: Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific islands — deep Indigenous histories and the last regions of the world settled by humans.
